name: Curried Chicken Dinner
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the sweet red pepper and green pepper into seeded and julienne strips.
2. Combine 2 1/2 cups water, rice, and bouillon powder in a medium saucepan; bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer until liquid is absorbed, about 50 minutes.
3. In a large Dutch oven, combine chicken, 2 1/2 cups water, curry powder, and salt. Bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer until chicken is tender, about 30 minutes. Drain the chicken.
4. Combine orange juice, cornstarch, sherry, and ginger in a small bowl; stir until cornstarch is dissolved.
5. Coat a large nonstick skillet with Pam. Place over medium-high heat until hot. Add celery and peppers, and saute until crisp-tender. Stir in the reserved chicken and orange juice mixture; bring to a boil. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly, until thickened.
6. To serve, spoon chicken mixture over hot, cooked rice.
PER SERVING: 332 calories, 32.1 g protein, 4.5 g fat, 39.3 g carbohydrates, 78 g cholesterol, 2.2 mg iron, 409 mg sodium, 51 mg calcium.
